notes_claims.text: Guillain-Barré syndrome (GBS) is the most common cause of acute flaccid paralysis globally, characterized by its sporadic and unpredictable nature

references.text: Leonhard SE, Mandarakas MR, Gondim FAA, et al. Diagnosis and management of Guillain–Barré syndrome in ten steps. Nat Rev Neurol . 2019;15(11):671-683. doi:10.1038/s41582-019-0250-9

notes_claims.text: GBS knows no boundaries—it can strike individuals of any age, anywhere, potentially leading to serious long-term physical and mental health consequences or even death

references.text: Leonhard SE, Mandarakas MR, Gondim FAA, et al. Diagnosis and management of Guillain–Barré syndrome in ten steps. Nat Rev Neurol . 2019;15(11):671-683. doi:10.1038/s41582-019-0250-9

notes_claims.text: GBS is classified as a post-infectious autoimmune peripheral nerve disease, often triggered by preceding infections such as Campylobacter enteritis and respiratory infections

references.text: Leonhard SE, Mandarakas MR, Gondim FAA, et al. Diagnosis and management of Guillain–Barré syndrome in ten steps. Nat Rev Neurol . 2019;15(11):671-683. doi:10.1038/s41582-019-0250-9

slide_claims.text: MARIPOSA: Evaluating the first and only 1L multitargeted combination in EGFR + mNSCLC vs osimertinib

references.text: RYBREVANT FASPRO ™ [Prescribing Information]. Horsham, PA: Janssen Biotech, Inc.
Cho BC et al; MARIPOSA Investigators. N Engl J Med . 2024;391(16):1486-1498.

slide_claims.text: MARIPOSA was a randomized, active-controlled, multicenter study and the largest phase 3 trial of patients with EGFR + disease *

references.text: RYBREVANT FASPRO ™ [Prescribing Information]. Horsham, PA: Janssen Biotech, Inc.
Cho BC et al; MARIPOSA Investigators. N Engl J Med . 2024;391(16):1486-1498.
Yang JC-H et al. Lancet Oncol . 2015;16(2):141-151.
Wu YL et al. Lancet Oncol . 2017;18(11):1454-1466.

Goldberg SB et al. J Thorac Oncol . 2025;20(suppl1):S86-S87. Please see Important Safety Information in this presentation. Please see the full Prescribing Information for RYBREVANT FASPRO ™, RYBREVANT , and LAZCLUZE available at this presentation. • MARIPOSA was a phase 3, randomized, open-label, multicenter study in 1,074 patients assessing the efficacy and safety of RYBREVANT (amivantamab-vmjw) in combination with LAZCLUZE compared with osimertinib in patients with treatment-naive, locally advanced or mNSCLC with EGFR exon 19 deletions or L858R substitution mutations
